title: Author guardrail + not-advice + provenance policy
context: Before any Ewing sarcoma trial-eligibility data is structured, the project must ratify the binding cancer guardrails as a written, citable policy. This is a childhood-cancer domain where a wrong 'you qualify / you don't' can cost a family a trial slot, so correctness and humility are treated as safety properties. The structured data is a research artifact and is never an eligibility determination or medical advice.
objective: Write the project's guardrail, not-advice, and provenance policy document that ratifies open-access-data-only, per-source license verification before ingest, 100% provenance on every assertion, the fixed not-advice/not-eligibility disclaimer string, and the HIGH-risk patient-facing blocking sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Policy ratifies the binding cancer guardrails: open-access/aggregate/de-identified data only; no patient-level or identifiable data; controlled-access (dbGaP/EGA) out of scope.
Defines the exact fixed not-advice + not-eligibility-determination disclaimer string carried on every record and release.
States provenance-on-every-assertion as a hard, schema-enforced gate (O2 = 100%); no assertion ships without full lineage.

States the refusal guardrails (no individualized advice, eligibility determination, prognosis, or trial ranking) and the patient-facing HIGH-risk BLOCKING sign-off (credentialed pediatric oncologist AND patient advocate, recorded by name/credential/date).
Requires per-source license verification before ingest; sources with unclear terms are excluded.
